title: What is an estimated bill and how does it work?
text: An estimated bill is issued when a meter reading is unavailable for the billing period. This happens when a meter reader cannot access your property, when a smart meter fails to transmit data, or during supply disruptions. The estimated consumption is calculated based on your historical usage patterns — typically your average kWh consumption over the same period in previous years. Estimated bills are clearly labelled 'Estimated' on the invoice. Once an actual meter reading is obtained (either by a meter reader visiting your property or by you submitting a self-read), the difference between the estimated and actual consumption is settled on the next bill. If actual consumption was higher than estimated, you will see a catch-up charge on the following bill. If actual consumption was lower, you will receive a credit. You can submit a self-read meter reading at any time through the customer portal or by calling customer service. Providing regular meter readings reduces the chance of estimated bills and ensures you are billed accurately. If you receive multiple consecutive estimated bills, please contact customer service to arrange a meter inspection.
